li.p: Find the node's physical machine and service from catalog node

ol.li:
Open the node's configuration file,find the "dbpath" and "indexpath".By default,the value of them are the same.
$ cat /opt/sequoiadb/conf/local/11820/sdb.conf svcname=11820 dbpath=/tmpfs/data1 diaglevel=5 transactionon=true role=data catalogaddr=vmsvr1-rhel-x64:11803

Connect to coord ,and stop node $ ~/sequoiadb/bin/sdb >db=new Sdb('192.168.20.35',11810) 192.168.20.35:11810 >shard=db.getShard('foo') foo >node=shard.getNode('vmsvr1-rhel-x64:11820') vmsvr1-rhel-x64:11820 >node.stop()

archive the files in the database and index pathes. $ tar -zcvf vmsvr1-rhel-x64.11810.2013_08_21.tar.gz /tmpfs/data1/*.dat /tmpfs/data1/*.idx /tmpfs/data1/foo.dat /tmpfs/data1/one.dat /tmpfs/data1/SYSTEMP.dat /tmpfs/data1/foo.idx /tmpfs/data1/one.idx /tmpfs/data1/SYSTEMP.idx

body.p: It is also applicable for catalog.

note.p: Please ensure the node is completely stopped before archiving data and index files,otherwise,the backuping files may be damaged.
